summary: Iter 21 — Validated the new _stripe_client(webhook_url) helper in /app/backend/server.py L455-475 that defends against the production sticky-state bug (stripe.api_base getting stuck on Emergent proxy across module-level state). Wrote 9 new pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_stripe_routing.py covering: (a) 4 unit tests that monkeypatch stripe.api_base + STRIPE_API_KEY to prove the helper deterministically resets api_base — including the CRITICAL sticky-state recovery scenario (api_base prepolluted to Emergent proxy + key swapped to sk_live_dummy → helper resets api_base to https://api.stripe.com); (b) 4 live HTTP regression tests covering POST /api/me/checkout (single + parametrized over all 4 payment methods), GET /api/payments/status/{sid} response shape (session_id/status/payment_status/amount_total/currency/fulfilled/plan), POST /api/webhook/stripe reachability; (c) 1 backend-log assertion confirming the new logger.info '[checkout] user=... plan=... method=... key_prefix=... api_base=...' line appears in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log with api_base=https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe (correct for sk_test_emergent key). All 9/9 PASS. Ran 38 regression tests across test_payment_methods.py + test_subscription.py + test_prefs.py — all 38/38 PASS, no regressions from the helper refactor. Frontend smoke: registered fresh user via API, opened Account dashboard via [data-testid=account-button], plan-monthly-card + plan-annual-card + pay-card-button + pay-link-button all visible, pay-link-button opens [data-testid=payment-link-modal] with valid https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_... URL in [data-testid=payment-link-input], Apple Pay/Google Pay buttons correctly hidden in headless Chromium, close button dismisses modal cleanly — all assertions PASS.
critical_code_review_comments: _stripe_client (server.py L455-475) — the fix is correct AND minimally invasive. Single-source-of-truth helper called at all 3 sites (POST /me/checkout L491, GET /payments/status L591, POST /webhook/stripe L623). Verified by monkeypatch tests that the helper correctly toggles stripe.api_base based on whether 'sk_test_emergent' is in STRIPE_API_KEY — including recovery from a sticky Emergent-proxy state when the key rotates to sk_live_. This is the exact bug pattern producing the BLANK Stripe Checkout page in production.

Diagnostic logger.info (L497-502) — emits api_base, key_prefix, plan, method, user email. Confirmed visible in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log. In production with sk_live_ the operator should see 'api_base=https://api.stripe.com' — if it ever shows the Emergent proxy with a live key, that's the smoking gun for the original bug.

Defense-in-depth note: the fix correctly forces api_base on EVERY request rather than relying on first-call routing, which is the only reliable defense against the upstream library's module-level singleton mutation pattern (emergentintegrations checkout.py L105-109).

Minor opportunity (non-blocking): the duplicate `import stripe as _stripe` inside both the helper (L469) and create_checkout (L496) is fine for readability but could be a module-level import. Not worth changing.
No FAILING assertions. Fix is production-ready.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_stripe_routing.py (NEW — 9 tests: 4 unit (monkeypatch sticky-state), 4 live HTTP regression, 1 log diagnostic)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (smoke: plan selector, pay buttons, payment-link modal with real Stripe URL, AP/GP hidden, close — all pass)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@example.com / admin123. STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent (untouched). Fresh TEST_ckhelper_*/TEST_cklog_*/TEST_pmsmoke_* users created per run.
seed_data_creation: None permanent. Test users self-clean naturally (TEST_ prefix; preview DB is non-production).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The Stripe routing fix is fully verified. New regression file at /app/backend/tests/test_stripe_routing.py — RUN IT for any future Stripe/checkout-related changes; the TestStripeClientHelperRouting class is the canonical guard against any future regression of the sticky-state bug. Important pytest detail: tests in /app/backend/tests/*.py import server.py via `sys.path.insert(0, '/app/backend')` + `import server as srv` (NOT `from backend import server`) because /app/backend has no __init__.py. pytest.ini pins -n 2 --dist loadscope which works fine with the new class-grouped tests. The diagnostic log line format is fragile to changes — if main agent edits the logger.info string in /me/checkout, update test_checkout_emits_api_base_log_line accordingly. Frontend payment UX testids are stable (see iter 20 context).
rca of the issue: Original bug: emergentintegrations/payments/stripe/checkout.py L105-109 mutates the module-level stripe.api_base singleton when STRIPE_API_KEY contains 'sk_test_emergent' but never resets it for non-emergent keys. Because Python module state persists for the whole uvicorn worker lifetime, ANY prior sk_test_emergent call (even from a startup probe, a test, or a brief .env placeholder) would leave stripe.api_base stuck pointing at https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe. Subsequent sk_live_ calls would then create Checkout Sessions on Emergent's proxy account, returning a session URL whose ID does not exist in the user's real Stripe account — Stripe Checkout therefore loads the skeleton, fails to fetch the session, and renders BLANK. Fix: server.py::_stripe_client(webhook_url) explicitly sets stripe.api_base before every StripeCheckout instantiation, choosing the Emergent proxy iff the key contains 'sk_test_emergent' else https://api.stripe.com. All 3 call sites refactored. Tests prove deterministic recovery from a pre-polluted sticky state.
